query: Five food companies sponsored a local food bank. Foster Farms donated 45 dressed chickens; American Summits donated twice the number of bottled water than the number of dressed chicken donated by Foster Farms; Hormel donated three times the number of dressed chickens that Foster Farms donated; Boudin Butchers donated one-third of the number of dressed chickens that Hormel donated; Del Monte Foods donated 30 fewer bottles of water than American Summits. How many food items did the companies donate in total?

nodes.task: Calculate the number of dressed chickens donated by Hormel given that it is three times the number of dressed chickens donated by Foster Farms.
Calculate the number of bottled water donated by Del Monte Foods given that it is 30 fewer than the number of bottled water donated by American Summits.
Calculate the number of dressed chickens donated by Boudin Butchers given that it is one-third of the number of dressed chickens donated by Hormel.
Calculate the total number of food items donated by all companies by summing the number of dressed chickens and bottled water donated by each company.
Search for the number of bottled water donated by American Summits, which is twice the number of dressed chickens donated by Foster Farms.
